title: Committed-secrets scan result: CLEAN (no secrets/PII)
evidence: gitleaks 0 leaks across 65 commits; builtin scanner 518 candidates all false-positive (508 high-entropy prose/hashes, 8 intentional author/test emails); saved secrets-scan.json
impact: No committed secret or leaked PII found

title: scan_secrets.py does not skip workflow-artifacts/ or *-lock.json (avoidable FP noise)
evidence: SKIP_DIR_NAMES (scan_secrets.py:94) lacks workflow-artifacts; SKIP_EXTS has .lock but not -lock.json; 91 of 518 candidates were in workflow-artifacts/ (incl. its own prior scan.json), 49 in package-lock.json
impact: Higher false-positive rate; scanner re-scans its own prior output on framework-using repos
recommended_action: Add workflow-artifacts to SKIP_DIR_NAMES and skip *-lock.json/*.lock.json generated lockfiles
validation: re-run scanner; count drops; add self-test

title: setup_tools.py lacks --version flag (inconsistent with the other 3 tools)
evidence: scan_secrets.py/run_checks.py/bench_env.py all have --version + _framework_version(); setup_tools.py main() (line 163) has only --format/--install
impact: Minor inconsistency; a user cannot query the framework version from this tool

title: scan_secrets.py computes shannon_entropy twice per high-entropy token
evidence: scan_secrets.py:204 (threshold check) and :209 (stored in extra) both call shannon_entropy(tok)
impact: Trivial redundant compute; no correctness impact
recommended_action: Compute once into a local and reuse
validation: code reads once; tests still pass
